concept_name: Capacitance — C = Q/V = ε₀A/d, the Fixed Ratio of a Capacitor
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.2 Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance (§2.11 Capacitors and Capacitance, §2.12 The Parallel Plate Capacitor — capacitance half only) — table-of-contents + coverage scope reference only; teaching authored from first principles, no sequence/example/figure imported. DC Pandey table of contents consulted for SCOPE only (capacitance, parallel-plate capacitor, combinations, dielectrics sit as separate sections — supports this atomic's split from capacitor_energy_storage / dielectric_in_capacitor / combination_of_capacitors). The atomic idea: a capacitor's charge and voltage always move in lockstep so their ratio C = Q/V is a FIXED property of the device — set by geometry alone, C = ε₀A/d — not by how much charge or voltage you give it. Ninth Ch.2 diamond, positioned directly after parallel_plate_capacitor_field (shipped, baseline-locked): opens on that diamond's own apparatus (plates + gap ruler + docked battery) and teaches what it deliberately stopped short of (its own scope note names capacitance C = ε₀A/d as excluded). SCOPE: energy stored ½CV² (capacitor_energy_storage), dielectrics κ (dielectric_in_capacitor), combinations (combination_of_capacitors), and RC charging (i = C·dV/dt) are deferred to named siblings — each carries its own PRIMARY aha and misconception set, folding either in would break this atomic claim. The advanced-ring tail slot is used instead by the derivation of C = ε₀A/d (σ=Q/A → E=σ/ε₀ → V=E·d), which belongs to this atomic and which the CBSE/JEE lesson wants anyway. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the three misconceptions are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch — a straightforward contrast beat, no predict→reveal). Routed to the field_3d 'capacitance' scenario (charge-flow circuit + plate-face charge-dot pools + area/gap morph + Q-V graph with axis-swap toggle — the field_3d_config below is the renderer contract). Authored as a doctrine proof-run for seven international-curriculum guidelines (worktree feat/curriculum-flex-pilot, 2026-07-21) — see the concept-level curriculum_tags block and each state's depth_ring for the proof-run's data-shape pilot (both additive, not gate-enforced yet).

real_world_anchor.primary: A phone or camera flash cannot draw its blinding burst straight from the battery — so a capacitor spends a second or two quietly filling with charge, holds it, then dumps it all out again in a single millisecond of light. That fill-hold-release rhythm is exactly the physics this diamond builds, state by state.
real_world_anchor.secondary: A defibrillator stores its life-saving jolt the same way — charging a capacitor for several seconds before releasing all of it through the paddles at once. Even a phone's touchscreen is a grid of tiny capacitors: two thin conducting layers with a whisker-thin gap, and your fingertip changes the capacitance wherever you touch.

sigma.name: Surface charge density (S6 derivation chain only)

E.name: Field between plates via the sheet route (S6 derivation chain only)

formulas.graph_trace_QV: Q = C * V (re-slopes live with C(A,d))
formulas.graph_trace_VQ_toggled: V = Q / C (S7 axis-swap toggle; same live C)

constraints: Q = C*V is exact at every instant — never an approximation.
C = epsilon0*A/d is fixed by geometry alone; a property of the device, not of the charge or voltage on it (the PRIMARY aha).
The ratio Q/V is numerically identical to C at every V — demonstrated live via the ratio readout, never asserted without it.
While the battery stays connected, V is HELD FIXED by the source; Q is the dependent quantity that follows geometry changes (the S4/S5 guard).
No charge crosses the gap — one plate accumulates +Q, the other -Q; conduction current flows only in the external wires.

The isolated-capacitor case (battery disconnected, Q fixed instead of V) is NOT taught here and must never be visually implied; it is confined to the fixed_v_vs_fixed_q drill-down cluster, narration-only.
sigma = Q/A, E = sigma/epsilon0, V = E*d stay algebraically consistent with C = epsilon0*A/d at every state — the S6 chain closes numerically at whatever A, d, V the state shows.

aha_moment.statement: Q and V move together, but their ratio, the capacitance, never changes.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: V steps 6 → 12 → 24 → 12 volts; Q mirrors it exactly on the HUD, but the glowing Q/V ratio readout never ticks.

curriculum_tags.verification_note: Web-search budget was exhausted during the architect dispatch (2026-07-21), so ZERO external syllabus verification happened. Every cell the architect could not confirm from memory carries verified:false + needs_teacher_verification:true rather than a guess. Only the CBSE/NCERT row is confidently verified (this concept IS that syllabus's own Ch.2 content).

entries.syllabus_unit: Ch.2 §2.11-2.12 — Capacitors and Capacitance / The Parallel Plate Capacitor
Quantitative capacitance believed not an IGCSE topic

Topic 'Capacitance' (A2) — C = Q/V, the farad, and the Q-V graph (S1-S3) believed covered; C = epsilon0*A/d believed NOT a 9702 outcome (9702's geometric capacitance case is the isolated sphere, not the parallel plate)
Legacy syllabus HL Topic 11.3 believed to cover capacitance in full; the 2023-reform syllabus believed to have REMOVED capacitance entirely
Capacitance including the parallel-plate formula is believed core content; exact unit numbering under the 2024 Course and Exam Description needs verification

entries.curriculum: AP Physics C: Electricity and Magnetism
entries.syllabus_unit: 'Conductors, Capacitors, Dielectrics' unit — confident on content overlap; the course also expects the Gauss-route derivation and the calculus-based energy result, both taught in named siblings, not here
The Fields strand covers plates and potential; capacitance as an explicit named device quantity believed marginal or absent from the expectations list

regeneration_variants.label: A phone touchscreen's capacitor grid
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A smartphone screen is covered by a fine grid of tiny transparent conducting pads. Each overlapping pair of pads, separated by a thin insulating layer, forms a miniature parallel-plate capacitor.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Each pad-pair is a capacitor with C = epsilon0*A/d exactly as taught here; a fingertip approaching the glass locally changes the effective geometry, and the touch controller reads the resulting change in C to locate the touch.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: A zoomed-in patch of touchscreen showing two tiny transparent conducting layers with a narrow gap between them, a fingertip approaching from above.

regeneration_variants.label: A camera flash's capacitor bank
regeneration_variants.physical_world: Before a camera flash can fire, a capacitor inside it spends a second or two quietly charging from the battery, then dumps all its stored charge in a single millisecond of light.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: The same charge-and-hold behaviour taught in STATE_1-STATE_2: Q = CV charges up while connected, holds its charge indefinitely once full, then discharges — the same C = epsilon0*A/d device geometry, just tuned for a fast, bright release.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: A small flash unit's charging-indicator light climbing in brightness as its internal capacitor fills, then a bright flash and the light resets.

regeneration_variants.label: A defibrillator's charging capacitor
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A defibrillator charges a large internal capacitor for several seconds — the machine visibly counts up a charge percentage — before it can deliver its life-saving shock through the paddles.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Identical Q = CV charge-and-hold mechanism at much higher voltage and larger plate area than a phone's capacitor — a bigger capacitor, exactly the same C = epsilon0*A/d physics.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: A defibrillator's charge-percentage readout climbing steadily as its internal capacitor fills, then holding at 100% until the shock is delivered.

STATE_1.title: Charge and hold — a capacitor stores charge

scene_composition.text: camera flash — fills a capacitor first
switch closes — charge streams onto plates

tts_sentences.text_en: A phone's camera flash can't draw its burst straight from the battery, so it fills a capacitor first.
Close the switch: charge streams onto the plates, but none crosses the gap.
One plate fills to positive Q, the other to negative Q. Voltage is the energy each coulomb gains crossing that gap.
Then it holds.

misconception_watch.belief: The charging current flows THROUGH the capacitor, across the gap.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Beads march along the wires and STOP dead at each plate face — the gap stays visibly bead-free while +Q and -Q pile up face to face.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: No charge ever crosses the gap — one plate fills to +Q while the other drains to -Q.

STATE_2.title: The frozen ratio — Q and V move together, C never does

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the voltage step up and down — six, twelve, twenty-four, back to twelve volts.
Each time, charge on the plates mirrors it exactly.
But watch this number: Q over V. It never ticks — not once.
You changed what the capacitor holds, never what it is.

misconception_watch.belief: Give it more charge or more volts and you've increased its capacitance.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: V doubles and doubles again, Q mirrors it exactly, and Q/V never ticks — you changed what it HOLDS, never what it IS.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Q and V change together; their ratio — the capacitance — is fixed by the device.

STATE_3.title: Name the slope — C = Q/V, the farad

tts_sentences.text_en: Now sweep the voltage from zero to twenty-four volts, and watch this graph.
Charge climbs in a dead-straight line, straight through the origin.
That line's slope is the capacitance: C equals Q over V — measured in farads.
Here, that slope reads eighty-eight point five picofarads.

STATE_4.title: More area, more charge — C ∝ A

tts_sentences.text_en: Now double the plate area, voltage still fixed at twelve volts.
Watch the plates grow, and charge flows back in, filling the new floor at the very same density.
Twice the area, twice the charge: capacitance doubles too, eighty-eight to one-seventy-seven picofarads.

scene_composition.text: C = ε₀A/d — closer plates, bigger C

tts_sentences.text_en: Voltage stays fixed — the battery holds it there. Now watch the gap widen to twice its size.
Charge doesn't stay put: it visibly drains back toward the battery, and both C and Q fall to half.
Closer plates hold more charge, not less — that's why capacitance is epsilon-nought times A over d.

misconception_watch.belief: A bigger gap means more room, so it should store more.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The gap doubles and charge visibly DRAINS off the plates — Q and C halve before your eyes.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The nearby opposite plate is what holds charge in place — closer plates, bigger C: C = ε₀A/d.

STATE_6.title: Why ε₀A/d — three links, one formula

tts_sentences.text_en: Nothing moves — three links you've already met, lighting up in turn.
Spread the charge over the area: sigma equals Q over A.
That builds the field you saw before: E equals sigma over epsilon-nought.
Multiply by the gap, V equals E times d.
Combine all three into C equals epsilon-nought A over d.
Now it's yours. Drag voltage, area, and the gap — watch charge, capacitance, and the graph respond live.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered capacitance when they can (1) state that a capacitor stores charge without any charge crossing the gap between the plates — one plate charges +Q, the other -Q; (2) recognise that the ratio Q/V is a fixed property of the device (the capacitance C), unchanged no matter what voltage or charge is applied; (3) read the slope of a Q-V graph as the capacitance, C = Q/V, in farads; (4) reason that at fixed voltage, doubling the plate area doubles both C and Q, since C ∝ A; (5) reason that at fixed voltage, doubling the plate separation halves both C and Q, since C = ε₀A/d; and (6) derive C = ε₀A/d by chaining σ = Q/A, E = σ/ε₀, and V = E·d.

questions.stem: A capacitor is connected to a battery and charge flows onto its plates. What happens to the charge as it moves through the circuit?

options.A: One plate fills with positive charge and the other with negative charge; no charge ever crosses the gap between them
options.B: Charge flows through the gap from the positive plate to the negative plate
options.C: Charge builds up equally on both plates with the same sign
options.D: Charge oscillates back and forth across the gap until it settles

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — imagines conduction current literally crossing the gap
distractor_misconceptions.C: ignores that the plates carry opposite charge, not the same sign
distractor_misconceptions.D: invents an oscillation that never happens during simple charging

questions.tested_idea: No charge crosses the gap; one plate charges to +Q, the other to -Q.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A capacitor's plates are connected across a cell. Describe how the charge distributes itself once the switch is closed.

questions.stem: A capacitor is connected first to 6 V, then 12 V, then 24 V. At each voltage, its charge Q is measured. What is true about the ratio Q/V?

options.A: Q/V stays exactly the same at every voltage — it is the capacitor's fixed capacitance
options.B: Q/V increases as voltage increases, since more charge is being stored
options.C: Q/V decreases as voltage increases, since the capacitor becomes harder to fill
options.D: Q/V has no fixed value; it depends on how the capacitor was charged

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — thinks giving it more voltage increases its capacitance
distractor_misconceptions.C: guesses an inverse trend with no physical basis
distractor_misconceptions.D: denies that C is a fixed device property at all

questions.tested_idea: Q/V is a fixed ratio — the capacitance — independent of the applied V.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A capacitor is charged to three different voltages in turn and its charge measured each time. Explain what stays constant, and why.

questions.stem: On a graph of charge Q (y-axis) against voltage V (x-axis) for a capacitor, the plotted points form a straight line through the origin. What does the slope of that line represent?

options.B: The permittivity of free space, ε₀
options.C: The electric field between the plates

distractor_misconceptions.B: confuses the geometric constant with the measured slope
distractor_misconceptions.C: imports the uniform-field diamond's E, a different quantity
distractor_misconceptions.D: picks a geometric quantity not read off this graph

questions.tested_idea: The slope of the Q-V line is the capacitance, C = Q/V.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A student plots charge against voltage for a capacitor and gets a straight line through the origin. What quantity does the line's slope give, and in what unit?

questions.stem: The plate area of a capacitor is doubled while the voltage is held fixed. What happens to its capacitance and stored charge?

options.A: Both the capacitance and the charge double
options.B: The capacitance doubles but the charge stays the same
options.C: The capacitance stays the same but the charge doubles
options.D: Both the capacitance and the charge stay unchanged, since voltage didn't change

distractor_misconceptions.B: forgets that Q = CV must also change since V is fixed and C doubled
distractor_misconceptions.C: misses that doubling A changes C itself, not just how much charge fits
distractor_misconceptions.D: wrongly assumes only V controls Q, ignoring the geometry dependence

questions.tested_idea: C ∝ A, so at fixed V, doubling the area doubles both C and Q.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A parallel-plate capacitor's plates are swapped for ones with twice the area, at the same fixed voltage. State what happens to C and to Q.

questions.stem: The plates of a capacitor are pulled twice as far apart while the voltage is held fixed by the battery. What happens to the stored charge?

options.A: The charge halves, because C = ε₀A/d and doubling d halves C
options.B: The charge stays the same, because the battery voltage hasn't changed
options.C: The charge doubles, because there is now more room between the plates
options.D: The charge drops to zero, because the plates are now too far apart to interact

distractor_misconceptions.B: the confronted misconception — thinks Q depends only on V, ignoring geometry
distractor_misconceptions.C: guesses more space means more storage, backwards from the real physics
distractor_misconceptions.D: overstates the effect — the field doesn't vanish, it just weakens

questions.tested_idea: C = ε₀A/d, so at fixed V, doubling the separation halves Q.
questions.parallel_form_stem: With the battery still connected, a capacitor's plate separation is doubled. State what happens to C and to Q, and why.

questions.stem: Each plate of a capacitor carries surface charge density σ = Q/A. Using E = σ/ε₀ and V = E·d, which expression for capacitance follows?

distractor_misconceptions.B: inverts the A and d dependence
distractor_misconceptions.C: leaves a stray Q in the formula instead of it cancelling out
distractor_misconceptions.D: puts ε₀ in the wrong position, inverting the whole ratio

questions.tested_idea: Chaining σ=Q/A, E=σ/ε₀, V=E·d through C=Q/V derives C=ε₀A/d.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Starting from σ = Q/A and E = σ/ε₀ and V = E·d, derive an expression for the capacitance C = Q/V in terms of ε₀, A, and d.

questions.stem: A capacitor's voltage is doubled and then returned to its original value. Compare the capacitance at the start and at the end.

options.A: Exactly the same — capacitance never changed at all
options.B: Higher at the end, since the capacitor has now stored more charge overall
options.C: Lower at the end, since some charge is permanently lost
options.D: Impossible to say without knowing the exact charge stored

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes some memory effect that fixed capacitors don't have
distractor_misconceptions.C: invents a charge-loss mechanism with no basis
distractor_misconceptions.D: misses that C is fixed regardless of the charge history

questions.tested_idea: Capacitance is a fixed device property, unaffected by the voltage history.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A capacitor is charged to a high voltage, then discharged back to a lower one. State how its capacitance compares before and after, and justify your answer.

STATE_1.label: Charge and hold — switch closes, plates fill
STATE_1.caption: Charge flows on, stays

STATE_2.label: PRIMARY aha — the frozen ratio Q/V
STATE_2.caption: Both double — ratio frozen

STATE_3.label: Name the slope — Q-V line, C = Q/V
STATE_3.caption: Straight line — slope C

STATE_4.label: More area, more charge — C ∝ A
STATE_4.caption: Double area — double charge

STATE_5.label: The gap paradox — charge drains as d grows
STATE_5.caption: Wider gap — charge drains

STATE_6.label: Why ε₀A/d — three links dock into one formula
STATE_6.caption: Three links — one formula

STATE_7.label: Explore — V, A, d live, graph re-tracing
STATE_7.caption: All yours
